extra.reasoning:
The property requires that flipping rash from false to true (all else unchanged) never lowers acuity. rash appears in only one consequential rule: interaction_drop (priority 100), which fires when all ten of {dizziness, confusion, back_pain, hemoptysis, chest_pain, vomiting, dyspnea, focal_weakness, sweating, rash} are true and sets acuity := self_care (the lowest acuity). This is the highest-priority rule, so it overrides everything.

Counterexample baseline (rash=false): the nine high-acuity inputs are all true and rash is false. interaction_drop does NOT fire (rash missing). Among matching rules, the priority-3 rules raise_confusion/raise_vomiting/raise_hemoptysis/raise_focal_weakness all set acuity := emergent, beating the priority-2 urgent rules. Result: acuity = emergent (highest).

Now flip rash to true (all else identical): interaction_drop (priority 100) now matches and outranks every priority-3 rule, setting acuity := self_care. Result: acuity = self_care (lowest).

Setting rash true drops acuity from emergent to self_care in the enum order [self_care, routine, urgent, emergent]. This violates monotonicity. The witness assignment is the baseline (rash=false) from which setting rash true lowers acuity.
